title: neoachmandra japonica
intro: Neoachmandra japonica, also known as the Japanese club moss, is a type of ancient plant that belongs to the club moss family, thought to have remained relatively unchanged since the Devonian period. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and its anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Neoachmandra japonica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Neoachmandra japonica are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
